Madrid Protocol vs Direct Filing in China: Which Path Is Right for You?

Guide June 7, 2026 · 10 min read

Why China Uses "First-to-File" System? What to Do If Your Brand Gets Squatted

China's trademark system operates on a "first-to-file" principle — the first person to register a trademark owns it, regardless of who used it first. This creates significant risks for international brands, but there are effective strategies to protect your brand.

💡

Key Takeaway

Yes, China uses a "first-to-file" system. This means the first person to register a trademark owns it — regardless of who used it first. This creates significant risks for international brands, but there are effective strategies to protect your brand and recover squatted trademarks.

📋 Why China Uses "First-to-File" System

Historical Context

China adopted the "first-to-file" system when it established its modern trademark law in 1982. This system was chosen for several practical reasons:

1

Administrative Efficiency

Easier to verify filing dates than to prove first use

2

Legal Certainty

Clear ownership based on registration records

3

International Alignment

Follows the Paris Convention and Madrid Protocol standards

4

Economic Development

Encourages formal registration and brand protection

⚠️ Why This Matters for International Brands

The Risk: Competitors can register your brand name before you do. Trademark squatters actively monitor international brands. Once registered, the squatter has legal rights to the mark. Recovering squatted trademarks is difficult and expensive.

🛡️ Strategies to Prevent Trademark Squatting

1

Register Before Entering China

Best practice: File trademark application 1-2 years before market entry

2

Use the Madrid Protocol

File international application designating China through WIPO

3

Monitor Trademark Gazette

Watch for similar marks and file oppositions within 3-month period

📊 Case Study: Farmskins Brand Protection

The Problem

  • ✗ Competitors bidding on "Farmskins" brand keywords in Baidu ads
  • ✗ Brand traffic being diverted to competitors

The Solution

  • ✓ RTMCN filed trademark in Class 42 (software services)
  • ✓ Successfully stopped competitors from using brand
